mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations and producing positive forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings upward and pushing equity returns higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or the predictive Rank IC for this threshold regime falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand expansion. This joint supply-demand alignment lifts corporate profit margins and equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline INDPRO-only or HOUST-only signal in out-of-sample walk-forward tests, or forward returns show no statistical difference from zero when both series rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of rising inflation expectations, equity markets initially price in higher nominal growth, supporting positive forward returns over the next month until the Federal Reserve responds with policy tightening.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2pp MoM, or the signal's predictive power vanishes once FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, indicating the regime shift has already occurred.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real GDP indicates expanding economic activity, which directly correlates with higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no positive correlation with subsequent 5-day SPX returns, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.01 over multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds 100, manufacturing capacity utilization is high, signaling strong corporate output and supporting positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO crosses above 100, or the signal produces negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with stable unemployment rates indicate healthy labor market expansion without overheating. This balanced labor dynamic supports sustainable earnings growth and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal fails to generate positive predictive information beyond a PAYEMS-only model, or forward returns turn negative when PAYEMS growth exceeds 250k monthly.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid credit expansion, consumer spending accelerates, driving retail and service revenues upward. This demand surge supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon until credit growth decelerates.
failure_condition: Forward returns become negative when RSAFS growth exceeds 8% QoQ, or the signal's predictive power reverses sign once RSAFS peaks and begins declining.
